--- SOURCE 1: The Power of Partnership to Address Antimicrobial Resistance ---
URL: https://www.amrindustryalliance.org/mediaroom/the-power-of-partnership-to-address-antimicrobial-resistance/

SUMMARY:
Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) is a significant threat to global ... • Best practices for cross-disciplinary collaboration to address other global health issues.

--- SOURCE 2: Global health partnerships are essential for tackling antimicrobial ... ---
URL: https://pharmaceutical-journal.com/article/opinion/global-health-partnerships-are-essential-for-tackling-antimicrobial-resistance

SUMMARY:
International partnerships between healthcare organisations can be mutually beneficial in reaching global health targets relating to antimicrobial resistance.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SUMMARY:
This network is renowned for its innovative strategies and practices that promote the responsible use of antibiotics in healthcare settings. The

--- SOURCE 4: Antimicrobial resistance - World Health Organization (WHO) ---
URL: https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/antimicrobial-resistance

SUMMARY:
*   [Health Topics](https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/antimicrobial-resistance#) *   **Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) is one of the top global public health and development threats. To address AMR in a programmatic manner that puts people, their needs and equitable access to health services at the centre of the AMR response in the community, in primary care, secondary and tertiary care, and at national and/or subnational level, WHO has developed the People-centred approach to addressing antimicrobial resistance in human health and WHO core package of interventions to support national action plans. To help fill the major gaps in R&D for antimicrobials, vaccines and diagnostic tools, WHO works closely with organizations such as the Global Antibiotic Research & Development Partnership (GARDP), the AMR Action Fund, and the Combating Antibiotic Resistant Bacteria Biopharmaceutical Accelerator (CARB-X).

FULL CONTENT:
Antimicrobial resistance 

===============

--- SOURCE 5: Global collaborations in antimicrobial stewardship: All hands on deck ---
URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10127241/

SUMMARY:
Tackling antimicrobial resistance (AMR) through antimicrobial stewardship (AMS) interventions is a key objective within the World Health Organization



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://onehealthtrust.org/wp-content/uploads/2017/06/garp.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A world map highlights countries involved in the Global Antibiotic Resistance Partnership, with distinct color coding representing different phases of participation from 2008 to 2016.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://image1.slideserve.com/2658877/the-global-antibiotic-resistance-partnership-garp-l.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A diagram illustrating the Global Antibiotic Resistance Partnership (GARP) with a focus on developing actionable policies on antibiotic resistance in India, Kenya, South Africa, and Vietnam.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://1.bp.blogspot.com/-pHV5h6n2Mso/X29olPBFVHI/AAAAAAAAL20/EuSP9A-aLVoDaP0jUjLOA07a0ubIC3YZACLcBGAsYHQ/s1600/global-antibiotic-resistance-partnership.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A logo featuring a stylized star made of capsules with images of bacteria inside, accompanied by the words "Global Antibiotic Resistance Partnership" in blue and green text.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://onehealthtrust.org/wp-content/uploads/2017/06/garp_brand_v13_3.jpg
DESCRIPTION: Six capsules with a world map design surround the words "Global Antibiotic Resistance Partnership" in blue and green text.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://infectionsinsurgery.org/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/global-alliance-for-infections-in-surgery1765.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A chart highlights best practices for global partnerships in surgery to combat antimicrobial resistance, including following guidelines, prescribing antibiotics appropriately, and supporting surveillance.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: International partnerships are crucial in combating AMR, as they enhance resource sharing, unify efforts across borders, and facilitate coordinated responses to global health crises.
Exchanging best practices among countries can lead to improved antimicrobial stewardship, standardized protocols, and effective interventions, ensuring more robust healthcare systems worldwide.

Coordinated research initiatives can accelerate the development of new antimicrobials and diagnostics, addressing critical gaps in AMR management through collaborative innovation and resource allocation.
